sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 44:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 44:1
items.html: The expansion of upper light does not complete its clothing in the vessels at one time. That is because in any Guf, meaning in ten Sefirot that come from the screen downward, there is the issue of the refinement of the coarseness. At that time all the lights of the ten Sefirot of the Guf return to their root and there is a new coupling in Malchut of the Rosh, called the second expansion. This expansion fills the vessels of the Guf once more and completes the clothing of the self in the vessels of the upper one. At that point, she also elicits a new Partzuf outside herself. (Part 3, Chapter 12 and Inner Light, item 8)
